# Project name

One-sentence product description.

## Project status

State whether the project is experimental, active, maintained, deprecated or archived.

## Existing features

List only behavior verified in the source code.

## Planned features

List approved roadmap items separately from existing functionality.

## Screenshots or demo

Add sanitized product images or a public demonstration link.

## Architecture

Describe components, data flows, trust boundaries and important external services.

## Technology stack

List runtime, framework, database, infrastructure and supported versions.

## Requirements and local development

Document prerequisites, installation, environment configuration, database setup and startup commands.

## Environment variables

Link to `.env.example`; classify required, optional, secret and production-only values. Never include real credentials.

## Docker deployment

Document build, run, health check, migration and rollback procedures.

## API documentation

Link to OpenAPI, Swagger or other versioned API documentation.

## Testing

Document unit, integration, security and end-to-end test commands.

## Security

Link to `SECURITY.md`; do not accept vulnerability reports publicly.

## Contributing, license and support

Link to the relevant policy files and support channel.

## Roadmap

List approved milestones without presenting plans as existing features.
